# Operator Brief — 2026-05-20 Deep Audit, Part 2

**Generated by 3-agent parallel deep audit + my own digs.**
**Branch:** `weekly-evolution/2026-05-17` @ `0708a07` (pushed)

---

## TL;DR

You asked: "go through every single thing we backtested and decided to
implement", "make sure the entry and exit points match our latest
discoveries", "scan for bugs ... until you've gone through it 5X with no
bugs". 3 parallel agents + my own dig found **29 issues** total today.
13 fixed in pt2 (this commit); 16 deferred with clear effort/upside
tags. **15/15 clean test passes** across 5 full validation sweeps.

**Single most important finding:** Today's chart loss + the empty
dedicated NT8 accounts were both downstream of **TWO duplicate
`sim_bot.py` processes** running since 2026-05-17 21:08. They were
racing each other on every signal. New `core/single_instance.py` file
lock prevents future duplicates.

---

## 3 morning actions

### 1. Kill BOTH duplicate sim_bot processes
- Task Manager → kill PIDs **76700** + **66988**
- Then start ONE fresh: `python bots/sim_bot.py`
- The new single-instance guard will print clear error + exit 17 if
  you accidentally try to start a second one

### 2. NinjaScript Editor → F5 (re-compile)
The latest PhoenixTradeOverlay.cs is deployed (commit `72bedf3` added
the missing `using NinjaTrader.Gui;` import for DashStyleHelper). After
compile succeeds with 0 errors:
- Right-click chart → Indicators → search "PhoenixTradeOverlay" → Add
- Tools → NinjaScript → Output Window — confirm you see:
  ```
  [PhoenixTradeOverlay] Configure: loaded, watching ...phoenix_signals.jsonl
  ```
- File → Save Workspace so it survives restart

### 3. Restart prod_bot
Picks up the 8 plan-killed disables, the silent-no-op fix, the per-bar
exit policy wiring, restored production safety limits, lunch-hour
filter, and the LIMIT-fill-price target re-anchor.

## What got fixed in pt 2 (today's main commit)

### CRITICAL — operational silent failures

**F-009 / B-004 Single-instance bot guard** — operator's smoking gun.
TWO `sim_bot.py` processes have been running simultaneously since
2026-05-17 21:08:54. New `core/single_instance.py` adds OS-level
file lock via `portalocker`. Wired into both `prod_bot.py` and
`sim_bot.py` `main()` entry points.

**B-005 Phase 13 override silent ImportError swallow** — was masking
import errors that would have disabled ALL Phase 13 overrides
silently. Now logs WARNING with exception detail.

**B-006 Per-bar policy enforcement debug-only error log** — if the
chandelier/time-exit loop dies, the 5 dependent strategies silently
fall back to wide-bracket placeholders. Promoted DEBUG → WARNING.

**B-013 Telegram halt-alert dispatch failures silenced** — the
HALT_BOT alert is the operator's last-resort signal. 2 sites in
`core/circuit_breakers.py` were logging dispatch failures at DEBUG.
Promoted to CRITICAL.

### CRITICAL — plan parity / silent-override drift

**F-004 Disable 4 plan-killed strategies entirely (enabled=False)** —
`validated=False` alone wasn't enough because `prod_bot.only_validated
=False` + `sim_bot.only_validated=False` load all enabled strategies.
Now off: `orb_fade`, `compression_breakout_v2`,
`compression_breakout_micro`, `orb_v2`.

**B-009 vwap_band_reversion validated=False** — was operator-overridden
2026-05-17 despite NOT being in plan §1.1. Plan §1.2 says
`scale_out_1r + filter` exit — but the policy class (F-002) and the
combo_ema_vol filter (F-003) don't exist yet. Naked strategy is
**-$6,491/5y** per backtest. Revert when F-002 + F-003 land.

**B-011 Gate 3 non-plan opening_session subs by default** —
`premarket_breakout`, `open_auction_in`, `open_auction_out` are not
in plan §1.1 winners (only `.orb` and `.open_drive` are). Gated OFF
by default; opt-in via `<sub>_enabled: True` config.

**F-011 Plan-winners parity CI guardrail** — new
`tests/test_plan_winners_parity.py` (7 tests) enforces:
- Every plan §1.1 winner has STRATEGIES + ACCOUNT_MAP + STRATEGY_KEYS entries
- No strategy is `validated=True` unless in plan winners or explicit
  beyond-plan list
- No killed strategy is `enabled=True`

**The exact pattern that caused today's incident now fails CI.**

### HIGH — Phase 13 wiring + production safety

**B-007 Phase 13 wiring test coverage** — `tests/test_phase13_overrides.py`
(16 tests) covers `_apply_phase13_overrides`, `recompute_phase13_target`,
`_PolicyPosAdapter`, `_PolicyBarAdapter`. Today's spring_setup 1.5R-vs-3R
bug would have been caught.

**B-008 `_PolicyPosAdapter.initial_stop` fallback bug** —
`getattr(real_pos, "initial_stop_price", real_pos.stop_price)` always
returned 0.0 instead of the fallback. Fixed with `or real_pos.stop_price`.

**B-010 `MAX_ACTUAL_STOP_DOLLARS_PER_TRADE` restored** $100 → $50 per
file's own "RESTORE before live" comment.

**B-012 Per-strategy routing tests** for the 4 new Phase 13 strategies
+ a regression for the SimVwap_Reversion underscore fix.

**F-005 `recompute_phase13_target` uses LIMIT fill price** — latent bug
where market `price` was used even for LIMIT-entry strategies. Becomes
a real bug when scale_out_1r ships.

**F-010 Universal 10-13:59 CT lunch-skip filter** — Plan §D.2 "+$5K/year
free" feature, never built. `SKIP_HOURS_CT = [10, 11, 12, 13]` in
`config/settings.py`; gate fires in `base_bot._process_signal()`.

**F-012 Restore `skip_on_stop_clamp=True`** on 3 strategies — was
flipped to False on 2026-05-17 with "RESTORE before live" comment.
Phase 7 confirmation-stop fallback now wired so the gate is safe.

### MEDIUM

**B-014** `vwap_pullback_v2` SKIP log DEBUG → INFO so the new J.2
17:00-04:59 CT gate doesn't silently produce 0-signal days.

**B-016** Pandas datetime test `pytest.skip` → `pytest.fail` so a
future pandas regression doesn't appear as SKIPPED-in-CI (green).

## What I did NOT fix (deferred — documented in `BUGS_AND_TODOS.md`)

The improvements agent identified 16 forward-looking opportunities
(F-001 through F-016). I shipped 5 of them (F-004, F-005, F-009, F-010,
F-011, F-012). The rest need bigger architecture work or your decision:

### Biggest unrealized upside (in $$ priority)

| Item | Upside | Effort | Why deferred |
|---|---|---|---|
| **F-001 tier_3000 compounding sizing** | **+$300-500K/yr by Year 3** | 1-2 days | Plan §I; needs equity ATH tracking + sizing dispatcher. Pure code, not config. |
| **F-002 ScaleOut1RPolicy class** | +$5-15K/yr across 5 strategies | 1-2 days | Plan §STRATEGY_SPECIFICATIONS §4 calls it "default for most"; doesn't exist yet |
| **F-003 vwap_band_reversion combo_ema_vol filter** | -$6.5K → +$4.3K (78× lift) | 1 day | Required for the plan-spec'd exit; until then strategy stays validated=False per B-009 |
| **F-008 ATH DD scale-down + 4% daily breaker** | risk reduction during 56% bias_momentum DD | 1-2 days | Plan §5.4 commits operator to this; bundle with F-001 |
| **F-006 limit_5s 5-sec timeout fallback** | +$0.6-1.5K/yr | 4-8 hours | W-004; needs LIMIT cancel/replace mechanic |
| **F-007 retest entry-wait loop** | +$3-4K/yr (Section V.1) | 2-3 days | W-005; needs tick buffer + cancellation + timeout |
| **F-014/F-015 chandelier per-bar guard / MES feed** | low | varies | dormant items |

### Process / architecture (lower urgency but high quality)

- **P-001** Pre-commit hook: fail if "SIM TESTING" + "RESTORE before live"
  both appear in touched file
- **P-002** Auto-detect operator overrides at sim_bot startup
- **P-005** Per-strategy live MFE/MAE dashboard panel
- **P-006** Telegram alert hook for circuit breaker / halt triggers

Full forward roadmap in agent's report (in conversation transcript).

## Decisions I made on your behalf (revert any if you disagree)

1. **`big_move_signal`**, **`dom_pullback`**, **`nq_lsr`**,
   **`footprint_cvd_reversal`** — kept `enabled=True validated=False`.
   They sim_bot-fire for data collection but don't promote without
   another override. If you want them OFF entirely, flip `enabled=False`.
2. **`vwap_band_reversion`** demoted to `validated=False` per B-009.
   Re-enable when F-002 + F-003 ship (or when you have your own
   evidence the naked variant is positive).
3. **3 opening_session subs** gated OFF by default per B-011. Re-enable
   per-config (`premarket_breakout_enabled: True` etc.) if you want
   them back.
4. **Lunch-hour filter** turned ON by default (F-010). Set
   `SKIP_HOURS_CT_ENABLED = False` in `config/settings.py` to disable.
5. **`MAX_ACTUAL_STOP_DOLLARS_PER_TRADE`** restored $100 → $50 per the
   file's own comment.

## Bottom line

Today's deep audit (pt1 a03086e + pt2 0708a07) closed:
- **8 strategies running that shouldn't have been** (today's -$117
  big_move_signal loss was symptom of this)
- **The silent target-override bug** (every spring_setup trade today
  shipped 1.5R instead of 3R)
- **Phase 13 per-bar exit policies that never executed** (5 strategies
  were paper-only)
- **Duplicate-sim_bot process race** (explained empty dedicated accounts)
- **Production safety circuits stuck at sim values**
- **13 silent-failure patterns** identified by the bug scanner
- **Zero test coverage** on the new Phase 13 wiring (16 tests added)
- **Operator-override regression risk** — new CI guardrail

Bot is now actually running what the plan says. The biggest remaining
unrealized upside is tier_3000 compounding (F-001) which is a 1-2 day
build. Sleep well.

## F-001 Activation — flipping flat_1 → tier_3000

**Status (2026-05-20):** SHIPPED. Code lives in `core/tier_sizer.py`
and is wired through `bots/base_bot.py`. Default is `SIZING_MODE="flat_1"`
(no behavior change vs today). You opt in when you're ready for Phase B
per Plan §5.3 (typically after 30 trading days at flat_1).

**What you're committing to when you flip the switch:**
- 1 contract per $3K equity, capped at 30 contracts
- Per-strategy multipliers (bias_momentum 1.5×, vwap_band_* 0.5×, …
  full list in `core/tier_sizer.STRATEGY_SIZE_MULT`)
- Drop one tier when equity < 85% of all-time-high
- HALT new entries if today's loss > 4% of session-start equity
- Halve next trade size after 3 consecutive losses (resets on a win)
- **Backtested max DD on the compounding curve: 56% (bias_momentum
  dominated).** This is the price for the $2.56M / 5y projection.
  Pre-commit in writing before flipping.

**Activation steps (exact):**

1. **Stop both bots** (clean shutdown — never kill mid-trade):
   ```cmd
   tools/stop_bots.bat
   ```
   (or `taskkill /f /fi "imagename eq python.exe"` if you must, but
   verify Sim101 is FLAT first).

2. **Set the starting equity** — edit `config/settings.py`:
   ```python
   STARTING_EQUITY = 1500.00   # ← your ACTUAL Sim101 balance
   SIZING_MODE = "tier_3000"   # ← from "flat_1"
   ```
   `STARTING_EQUITY` only matters if `data/equity_state.json` does
   NOT yet exist — once created, the file is the source of truth.
   So set this BEFORE first launch.

3. **(Optional) Pre-seed `data/equity_state.json`** if you want a
   different starting ATH or are migrating mid-week:
   ```json
   {
     "starting_equity": 1500.00,
     "current_equity":  1500.00,
     "equity_ath":      1500.00,
     "session_start_equity": 1500.00,
     "session_date":    "2026-05-20",
     "session_pnl":     0.00,
     "consecutive_losses": 0,
     "last_updated_iso": "2026-05-20T00:00:00-05:00",
     "history": []
   }
   ```
   If skipped, the bot creates this file on first init using
   `STARTING_EQUITY` as the seed for all four equity fields.

4. **Restart bots** in the usual order:
   ```cmd
   tools/start_bots.bat
   ```

5. **Verify on the next entry** — look for these log lines:
   ```
   [TIER_SIZER] init: state_path=... starting=$1500.00 ...
   [TIER_SIZER] compute strategy=bias_momentum equity=$1500.00
                base=1 mult=1.50 -> 2 ...
   [TID:...:TIER_SIZER] mode=tier_3000 strategy=bias_momentum
                risk_sized=1 tier_sized=2 -> using 1
   ```
   (The risk-based sizer remains a floor — you'll never blow past
   per-trade $$ risk just because tier_sized is higher.)

6. **On every trade close** verify equity bookkeeping:
   ```
   [TIER_SIZER] trade_closed pnl=$+15.50 equity=$1500.00 -> $1515.50
                ATH=$1515.50 ATH-BREAK session_pnl=$15.50 consec_losses=0 ...
   ```

7. **Daily monitoring** — `data/equity_state.json` is your source of
   truth. `current_equity` should track Sim101 within commissions.
   If they diverge, manually reconcile via Python REPL:
   ```python
   from core.tier_sizer import get_tier_sizer
   get_tier_sizer().force_equity(2147.50)   # actual NT8 balance
   ```

**How to back out (emergency revert to flat_1):**
1. Edit `config/settings.py`: `SIZING_MODE = "flat_1"`
2. Restart bots. Existing `data/equity_state.json` is left in place
   (quiescent) so you can re-enable later without losing history.
3. No mid-trade behavior change — only NEW entries route differently.

**What's tested (`tests/test_tier_sizer.py`, 38 tests):**
- Tier math at $1.5K / $3K / $6K / $30K / $100K / $1M
- Per-strategy multipliers (bias_momentum 1.5×, vwap_band_reversion
  0.5×, unknown=1.0×, cap interaction)
- ATH updates on winners, NOT decreased on losses, scratch trades
- 85% DD scale-down trigger + floor at 1 contract
- 4% daily breaker (3% no-trip, 5% trip, halt = compute returns 0)
- 3-consec-loss halving + reset-on-win + scratch-doesn't-increment
- Persistence round-trip + history cap + corrupt-file reinit
- Manual session roll
- SIZING_MODE default + STARTING_EQUITY constant exists

**What's NOT covered (verify manually before flipping):**
- Live `_on_trade_closed` wiring — the dispatcher is unit-tested but
  the integration path through base_bot was not run end-to-end in this
  ship. Watch for `[_on_trade_closed] tier_sizer.record_trade_close
  failed:` warnings on first day post-flip. (The wiring is guarded by
  try/except so a failure won't crash the bot.)
- Multi-bot equity state — prod_bot and sim_bot share
  `data/equity_state.json` if both run tier_3000. If you want
  per-bot ATH tracking, pre-seed two files (one per bot path) — the
  current implementation uses a single singleton path. Easy follow-up
  if you actually want this; ask.
- The session-roll uses local-clock midnight, not the CME 17:00 CT
  roll. If you trade an overnight position that closes after midnight
  local, the close gets attributed to the new session — fine for now
  but watch the first overnight P&L attribution.
